name: Mobile General Medicine Clinic
description: The Mobile General Medicine Clinic provides primary care services that cover a wide variety of healthcare needs.

taxonomy.sub_category: Mobile General Medicine Clinic Updated on June 22, 2024 · Created on November 17, 2019 Mobile General Medicine Clinic Upcoming Update The Mobile General Medicine Clinic provides primary care services that cover a wide variety of healthcare needs. Developed By Odulair Tested By Mobile General Medicine Clinic Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Upcoming Update Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description Odulair’s Mobile General Medicine Clinic provides primary care services that range from routine health screenings, to check ups, to care for chronic conditions, to psychological consultations. The price starts from 150000 USD and increases as the customer introduces additional requests. Interview with manufacturer Target SDGs SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy Market Suggested Retail Price $150,000.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Public Sector Agencies, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations Odulair is a direct manufacturer. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Philips and Rhiza Foundation Mobile Clinic , Off Road Mobile Clinic , Clinic In A Can , and Mobile Schools Health . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method Odulair is a direct manufacturer. They build the mobile clinics that can be customized based on the client's requests and needs. Interview with manufacturer Intellectural Property Type Copyright User Provision Model Users need to follow the steps on the product's website Distributions to Date Status Unknown Devices/Products delivered Mobile clinics with all their needed equipment Maximum payload dimensions (LxWxH, m) Unknown Maximum payload mass (kg) Unknown Maximum speed (kph) Unknown Radius of Transport (km) Unknown Design Specifications The design is unique depending on the customer's needs and requests. Interview with manufacturer Technical Support The manufacturer offers all the necessary training to have the client's team up and running with the outreach medical services. They offer training in the following fields: Interview with manufacturer Equipment training Patient flow training Life safety equipment training Maintenance training Security training Replacement Components Medical equipment, HVAC equipment, solar panels, truck equipment Lifecycle 20 years Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters A world where everyone has access to sustainable healthcare. Vetted Performance Status None Safety The manufacturer provides safety equipment that include the following: Interview with manufacturer Fire detection system Fire suppression system Emergency lighting system Fire extinguishers Evacuation plan Complementary Technical Systems Truck, medical equipment, HVAC equipment, solar panels, generator and batteries Academic Research and References Adams, J. M., Flomo, K., Banville, K. S., & McCallum, S. C., 2015, Design of Mobile Dialysis Treatment , Worcester Polytechnic Institute Attipoe-Dorcoo, S., 2018, An Overview of Costs, Utilization, Geographical Distribution & Influence of Mobile Clinics in Rural Healthcare Delivery in the United States , Ph.D. Thesis, Public Health Department, The University of Texas School of Public Health 2016, Odulair Surpasses Samsung in Delivering World’s First 100-Percent Solar Powered Mobile Health Clinic to Clemson University , PR Newswire Osby, L., 2017, Clemson’s Mobile Health Clinic Powered by the Sun , Greenville News Compliance with regulations The clinic is built using the Guidelines for Design and Construction of Hospitals and Outpatient Facilities. Besides that, the clinic is complaint with regulations in the client's country and region Interview with manufacturer Evaluation methods After installation of the product, the manufacturer Interview with manufacturer Evaluates the mobile medical unit placement evaluation Evaluates the ability to maneuver the mobile medical unit Ensures proper mobile medical unit placement Reviews external utilities available for mobile medical unit connections: power, water, sewer, medical gas, telecommunications / data, fire suppression, security detection (alarm), fire detection (alarm), paging system, nurse call monitoring, medical gas monitoring and HVAC monitoring Evaluates the need of mobile medical unit tie-downs in consideration of weather extremes or seismic requirements Other Information Guidelines for Design and Construction of Hospitals and Outpatient Facilities .
